channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Philippine ex-President Duterte set to appear in Hague courtroom to face 'war on drugs' charges
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte is scheduled to make his first appearance before judges of the International Criminal Court on Friday, days after his stunning arrest in Manila on murder charges linked to the deadly “ war on drugs ” he oversaw while in office
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended: What you should know
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration's recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China, Russia and Iran call for end to US sanctions on Iran and the restart of nuclear talks
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Representatives of China, Russia and Iran called Friday for an end to U.S. sanctions on Iran over its rapidly advancing nuclear program and a restart to multinational talks on the issue. The talks are the latest attempt to broach the matter and come after U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Europe's military personnel shortfalls exposed as Trump warns US security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In the year after Russia launched outright war on Ukraine, NATO leaders approved a set of military plans designed to repel an invasion of Europe. It was the biggest shake-up of the alliance’s defense readiness preparations since the Cold War.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Big March storm system threatens US with tornadoes, blizzards and wildfire risk
item.description: A sweeping storm system moving across the U.S. threatens to bring tornadoes, blizzards and possibly wildfires to different regions of the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Once-routine immigration check-ins become high-stakes calculation as some are detained
item.description: When a couple from Colombia who was planning their wedding showed up for a check-in with U.S. immigration authorities, one was given his next appointment date. The other was detained and deported. Jhojan doesn’t know why Felipe was detained at the Feb.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, CEDAR ATTANASIO and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: University of Central Florida suspends 2 fraternities for alleged hazing, including hitting pledges with cars
item.description: An investigation is underway after two fraternities at the University of Central Florida, Sigma Chi and Delta Upsilon, were suspended over hazing allegations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: John Feinstein, one of country's foremost sports writers, dies suddenly
item.description: John Feinstein, one of the country's foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, including the groundbreaking “A Season on the Brink” about college basketball coach Bob Knight, died unexpectedly Thursday. Feinstein was 69.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man claims amnesia after St. Pete police say he rammed couple off road, killing them
item.description: The man accused of killing a woman and injuring her boyfriend faced a judge for the first time on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump pushes annexation of Greenland during meeting with NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday expressed confidence the United States would annex Greenland, even suggesting the head of the NATO alliance could be a key player in facilitating the acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration tariffs create uncertainty for brewery owner
item.description: Cary said Begyle Brewing imports about 200K pounds of barley from Canada each year and that tariffs would hurt the brewery's margins.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Federal student loan site down Wednesday, a day after layoffs gutted Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An hours-long outage Wednesday on StudentAid.gov, the federal website for student loans and financial aid, underscored the risks in rapidly gutting the Department of Education, as President Donald Trump aims to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pete Buttigieg declines Senate run in Michigan
item.description: Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg announced he will not run for Senate in Michigan, keeping the door open for a potential presidential run in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WGN 9

item.title: Explosion at Texas Tech causes evacuations, immediate spring break
item.description: An explosion at a substation on the Texas Tech campus has caused power outages at the Texas Tech and the Health Sciences Center Wednesday evening, according to a TechAlert.
item.dc:creator: Georgina Johnston, WGN 9

item.title: Lessons learned in Natalee Holloway disappearance apply to Sudiksha Konanki case: retired FBI agent
item.description: A former FBI special agent says investigators can use lessons learned from previous missing person's cases like Natalee Holloway's to find Sudiksha Konanki.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ex-Texas megachurch pastor indicted in Oklahoma on child sex abuse charges
item.description: A former pastor of a Texas megachurch who resigned after a woman accused him of sexually abusing her in the 1980s has been indicted in Oklahoma on child sexual abuse charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vaccinating poultry could help cut soaring egg prices, but the US is hesitant
item.description: Without a new policy including vaccines, the government will continue to slaughter every flock with a bird flu infection to limit the spread of the disease. Those deaths have totaled over 166 million birds in the U.S. since 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawsuit claims Girl Scout cookies contain 'heavy metals,' pesticides
item.description: A new federal lawsuit claims Girl Scout Cookies contain heavy metals, but the organization assures the cookies are safe.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, KTLA 5

item.title: A government program made tax filing free and more efficient. Musk and DOGE may get rid of it anyway
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mia Francis, a 22-year-old barista from Boston, filed her taxes on her own this year for the first time, using a free government tax filing program that made it easy because it did most of the work for her.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Immigrants fuel growth in major US urban counties
item.description: Immigrants kept the largest urban counties in the U.S. growing last year. Core counties in the Houston, Miami and Phoenix metropolitan areas grew more than any others in the country primarily because of people moving in from outside the United States, according to population estimates from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Emergency at California hospital a ‘swatting call': Authorities
item.description: The San Bernardino County Sheriff’s Department responded to Loma Linda University Children’s Hospital Wednesday evening following reports of a possible shooter. By 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Man arrested in Utah nursing home may not be extradited for 1977 Hawaii murder
item.description: A man who was arrested at a Utah nursing home in connection to a 1970s Hawaii murder may be released, according to a proposed court motion.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly 90 displaced after explosion at assisted living center: Denver officials
item.description: Nearly 90 people were displaced after an apparent explosion at an assisted living center Wednesday in southeast Denver that sent eight people to the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: A breakdown of major EPA deregulatory moves around water, air, climate
item.description: Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Lee Zeldin on Wednesday announced nearly three dozen deregulatory moves that he said would spur the U.S. economy by rolling back rules that have unfairly burdened industry.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, ALEXA ST. JOHN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks parts of Trump's executive order seeking to punish law firm Perkins Coie
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge blocked President Donald Trump's administration Wednesday from enforcing portions of an executive order designed to punish a prominent law firm linked to Democratic-funded opposition research during the 2016 presidential campaign into ties between the Republican can
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 4-year-old calls 911 to say 'bad mom' ate his ice cream and needed to go to jail
item.description: We've all been there. You go to the freezer and discover your ice cream has been eaten, and not by you.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas Strip hotel to be redesigned by reality TV star
item.description: Lisa Vanderpump announced Wednesday she plans to completely renovate a Strip hotel, transforming it into the "Vanderpump Hotel.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration drops lawsuit against company over alleged abuse at its child migrant shelters
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration moved to drop a civil lawsuit Wednesday against the largest provider of housing for migrant children over allegations of sexual abuse and harassment of unaccompanied minors, saying it also would no longer be using that company's services.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: New Haven police reconnect father with daughter who was kidnapped in 1999
item.description: New Haven police have reconnected a father and his daughter who was kidnapped decades ago from New Haven.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Taylor, KRON4

item.title: Study shows rain-soaking atmospheric rivers are getting bigger, wetter and more frequent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As extreme weather events have hit the world hard in recent years, one meteorology term — atmospheric rivers — has made the leap from scientific circles to common language, particularly in places that have been hit by them. That stands to reason.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: EU retaliates as Trump steel, aluminum tariffs take effect
item.description: After announcing the response, the EU released a 99-page list of possible items that could be subject to tariffs, including fruit and vegetables, meat, alcoholic beverages, nicotine vapes and chewing gum.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: The US is short nearly 4 million homes
item.description: Builders faced barriers like zoning and supply costs, which make producing affordable homes challenging.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, Fox 5

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, ABC 4

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's threat Tuesday to double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada led the provincial government of Ontario to suspend its planned surcharges on electricity sold to the United States.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4
